statement: The diagonal shifted-Xi inequality for every 0<omega<1/2 excludes all non-real zeros of Xi(z)=xi(1/2+i z).

zeroDescentContradictionStatus.label: zero-descent contradiction
zeroDescentContradictionStatus.reason: If Xi(rho)=0 with Im rho=b>0, then z=rho-i omega lies in C_+ for 0<omega<min(b,1/2), and the diagonal inequality forces Xi(rho-2i omega)=0 on a continuum, impossible for nonzero entire Xi.

realEntireConjugationStatus.label: real-entire conjugation exclusion
realEntireConjugationStatus.reason: Xi is real entire, so lower-half-plane zeros conjugate to upper-half-plane zeros, already excluded.

endpointZeroLocationStatus.label: endpoint zero-location theorem
endpointZeroLocationStatus.reason: All zeros of Xi in the z-normalization are real.

proof: For 0<omega<min(b,1/2), set z=rho-i omega so z lies in C_+.
The resulting vertical continuum of zeros has accumulation points in C.
A nonzero entire function cannot vanish on such a set; conjugation handles the lower half-plane.
